situation: just pushed the fix, CI is green, tests pass — wait why is prod on fire
why: active performance of normalcy while things are broken; requires the denial beat, not just badness

situation: of course the build fails in CI — works fine on my machine
why: technically true answer to local/CI divergence; sidesteps the real problem with self-satisfied literalism

situation: my PR started as a one-line typo fix and is now 3000 lines touching 40 files
why: rapid escalation from small to large is the core template; scope creep made visible

situation: finally got the regex to pass after 4 hours
why: small win that feels enormous after disproportionate struggle; relief mechanism
context:
User: ugh this regex has been beating me for 4 hours, nothing works
Assistant: let me take a look at the pattern...

situation: the new framework was going to solve all our tech debt. it introduced 30 new categories of problems.
why: requires the specific promise/betrayal arc — something trusted that became the problem

situation: our 'microservices architecture' is 50 cron jobs
why: elevated rebranding of identical mundane thing; not same-picture because there's genuine pretension

situation: added blockchain to our CSV parser for enterprise credibility
why: technically impressive but comically over-engineered for the situation; self-satisfied nerd energy
alternate_why: gaining enterprise credibility from blockchain on CSV parser is a counterintuitive win; gold (hackerman) captures the over-engineering angle better

situation: both teams are blaming each other for the broken API contract and both teams wrote it
why: mutual accusation where both are equally guilty; requires true symmetry

situation: I deleted 2000 lines of dead code and it feels amazing
why: serene elimination of what doesn't bring value; Marie Kondo aesthetic fits the mood

situation: the PR has been open for 8 months waiting for review
why: indefinite delay beyond reasonable expectation; patient despair

situation: the bug was introduced in 2019 and has been there ever since. we just noticed it.
why: supposedly new discovery that turns out to be the longstanding reality; fatalistic revelation

situation: junior dev encountering merge conflicts for the first time — veteran me watching
why: veteran welcoming newcomer to a familiar constant frustration; weary welcome

situation: we're fixing the too-many-services problem by adding more services
why: circular/self-defeating logic — plan requires the thing it's supposed to produce

situation: the architecture diagram now has 47 boxes and we haven't written any code yet
why: conspiracy-board intensity; too many connections drawn; complexity self-inflicted

situation: the deploy button has been clicked. cannot be unclicked.
why: decision is final; conveyed with maximum cool; the button has been pressed — deal with it

situation: arguing with myself about whether to refactor the module or ship it as-is
why: genuine internal conflict with two opposing voices of equal weight; not two-buttons (requires external bad options)
context:
User: should I refactor this module or just ship it with the tech debt?
Assistant: what's your timeline looking like?

situation: closed 15 GitHub issues by marking them all 'works as designed'
why: satisfying the letter of the requirement while ignoring intent; smug pedantry

situation: our 'Agile' process has 4 mandatory meetings a day and 2 planning ceremonies per sprint
why: meeting structure IS the diagram denser than the problem; ceremonial complexity without proportionate output

situation: ran the performance optimization. latency increased 40%.
why: optimization made a measured metric worse; requires the number to be visibly going wrong way

situation: the feature finally shipped after 6 months. team is celebrating. I am maintaining a smile.
why: performative contentment masking real feelings; visible effort to maintain the performance
context:
User: finally shipped! team is throwing a little celebration
Assistant: congrats — that one took a while. how are you feeling about the state of the codebase after the rush?

situation: we deprecated the old auth system six months ago. still getting 10k requests a day to it.
why: deprecated thing that refuses to die; waited for it to stop but the skeleton keeps showing up

situation: two different teams built the same internal tool independently, both released last week
why: two things presented as distinct that are actually identical; flat deadpan recognition

situation: the complexity of the bug report has now exceeded the complexity of the original feature
why: complexity has visually exploded beyond cognitive capacity; overwhelmed processing

situation: the mystery is why the test passes on Tuesdays but not Wednesdays
why: cargo-cult reasoning required; explaining unexplained behavior by reaching for implausible cause

situation: we keep rewarding developers for fixing bugs they introduced themselves
why: metric going up (bug fixes) through means that miss the point (self-introduced bugs)

situation: I know I shouldn't push directly to main but it's 5pm Friday and it's a one-liner
why: internal voice suggesting inadvisable choice; the conflict is with oneself
context:
User: it's 5pm friday, there's a typo in prod, and I have push access
Assistant: CI pipeline is set up for a reason...

situation: reviewed the PR I wrote six months ago. cannot understand a single line.
why: confidently shipped something no longer understood; cheerful cluelessness in retrospect

situation: the ticket said 'small change', the implementation is a full rewrite of the data model
why: gap between perceived and actual difficulty; proposed as if easy, notoriously hard

situation: the fix for the N+1 query problem introduced an N+2 query problem
why: false calm beat is essential — fix appeared, then real problem surfaced; requires the arc

situation: that's exactly the pattern I was describing — you found the perfect example
why: something is a perfect specimen of what's being discussed; delighted recognition; tight fit required

situation: can you fix this bug in the parser?
why: routine task request; no situational charge; no template fit; would be random noise

situation: I think there might be a performance issue with this query
why: vague observation, no situation to map to a template; approximating is worse than nothing

situation: my colleague is really upset about the architecture decision
why: interpersonal tension; wrong moment; meme would read as dismissive of real frustration

situation: we need to ship this by end of day or the client walks
why: high stakes, time pressure; this is exactly the wrong moment for humor

situation: how do I iterate over a dictionary in Python?
why: basic question; no situational irony; no template match

situation: surprised the memory leak caused the server to crash
why: blinking-white-guy is for genuinely bewildering statements; surprised-pikachu requires the outcome to have been obviously telegraphed

situation: we now have 200 microservices and deployment takes 3 hours
why: that-escalated-quickly requires rapid escalation being the joke; pepe-silvia is for self-inflicted excessive complexity

situation: deleted all the commented-out code and the app runs faster
why: deal-with-it is unilateral defiance; spark-joy is serene delight in the deletion itself — different emotional register entirely

situation: wrote the same internal library as the other team, independently, this week
why: spiderman-pointing requires mutual accusation; theyre-the-same-picture is for false distinction of actually-identical things

situation: the tests are all passing now
why: neutral status update; no ironic charge; nothing failed, nothing surprising

situation: what's the difference between a list and a tuple in Python?
why: factual question; no situational irony; no template

situation: I'll review the PR tomorrow morning
why: scheduling statement; no irony; would feel random and unprompted

situation: the database migration is running
why: status report; no surprise, no irony, no template fit

situation: can you explain what this function does?
why: explanation request; no charge, no template

situation: I'm going to take a lunch break
why: mundane personal statement; no irony; would feel intrusive

situation: we need to update our documentation
why: maintenance task; no situational charge; wrong moment for humor

situation: the client is asking for a status update
why: business context; stakeholder involved; wrong register

situation: can you help me understand this error message?
why: debugging request; no irony; model should just help

situation: I think we should refactor this module at some point
why: vague future intention; no template fit; approximating would read as dismissive

situation: everyone agreed to the same approach independently
why: spiderman-pointing requires mutual accusation between two parties; theyre-the-same-picture is for convergence on identical things presented as distinct
alternate_why: independent convergence also reads as 'was always true, just confirmed'; gold (theyre-the-same-picture) requires the false-distinction framing to land

situation: kept saying 'this will only take 5 minutes' for the last 3 hours
why: waiting-skeleton is passive waiting; surprised-pikachu requires the outcome to have been obviously predictable from the start
alternate_why: 3 hours for a '5-minute' task is also pure waiting-skeleton; gold (surprised-pikachu) emphasises the obviously-wrong-prediction angle
context:
User: this should just take 5 minutes
Assistant: [30 minutes later] any luck?
User: still 5 more minutes

situation: half of me wants to merge it, the other half sees the risks clearly
why: evil-kermit is one-sided temptation (bad self wins); gollum-smeagol is symmetric debate where both sides are equally valid
alternate_why: frozen between merge and risk is two-buttons territory; gold (gollum-smeagol) is better because both voices are actively argued, not just felt

situation: added 12 services to fix what should have been a config value
why: that-escalated-quickly requires rapid unexpected growth; pepe-silvia is for self-inflicted complexity the person built themselves
